crisis: International displacement from Syria to Lebanon

tavily.answer: A ceasefire largely holds in Lebanon, but fears persist it may collapse. The conflict has led to significant displacement, with many Lebanese civilians affected. The International Labour Organisation reports that the private-sector workforce in Lebanon has been cut by one-third due to the war, with severe unemployment rates in southern regions. The ongoing conflict has also endangered Lebanon's World Heritage Sites, with significant destruction reported in the southern part of the country. The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) faces an uncertain future as its mandate is set to expire, raising questions about the country's security and humanitarian situation.
